title: PDF/A (Portable Document Format Archive)
abstract: An ISO-standardized version of PDF specialized for the long-term digital preservation of electronic documents.

title: XML (Extensible Markup Language)
abstract: A flexible, human-readable text format used to define and structure complex datasets and hierarchical information.

title: TIFF (Tagged Image File Format)
abstract: A flexible, industry-standard raster format heavily utilized in professional archiving and high-end photography.

title: TSV (Tab-Separated Values)
abstract: A plain-text file format used for storing tabular data, separating values strictly with horizontal tabs.

title: PNG (Portable Network Graphics)
abstract: A popular raster graphics format that utilizes lossless compression and supports alpha channel transparency.

title: JPEG (Joint Photographic Experts Group)
abstract: A widely-used lossy image format optimized for compressing continuous-tone photographic imagery.

title: JPEG 2000 (Lossless Part 1 / Core Coding)
abstract: An advanced image compression standard based on wavelet transform technology, offering both lossy and lossless coding pathways.

title: SVG (Scalable Vector Graphics)
abstract: An XML-based vector image format used for defining two-dimensional graphics with support for interactivity and animation.

title: PSD (Adobe Photoshop Document)
abstract: A proprietary raster and vector graphics format developed by Adobe to preserve advanced image editing states and project layers.

title: CSV (Comma-Separated Values)
abstract: A widely used plain-text format for tabular data, using commas to separate field values.

title: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation)
abstract: A lightweight data-interchange format that is easy for humans to read and write, and easy for machines to parse.

title: MP3 (MPEG-1 Audio Layer III)
abstract: A digital audio format using lossy data compression, widely used for music storage and streaming.

title: MP4 (MPEG-4 Part 14)
abstract: A digital multimedia container format most commonly used to store video, audio, and subtitles.
